case.title: CITY OF PLANTATION v. UTILITIES OPERATING CO., INC.

opinion.h4: CITY OF PLANTATION v. UTILITIES OPERATING CO., INC., (1964)
CITY OF PLANTATION v. UTILITIES OPERATING CO., INC. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF FLORIDA. No. 72. Decided October 12, 1964.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
William E. Miller and Robert J. Corber for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
DAVIS v. NEELY ET UX. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OKLAHOMA. No. 68. Decided October 12, 1964.
Thomas D. Finney, Jr., and Grant W. Wiprud for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
